helper_anchor.preserved_role: Previously observed generic helper called by the 0x19E90-driven 'totp'/'login' consumer family.

fresh_helper_structure.role: Concrete entry-builder/finalizer that copies an incoming label string, seeds a shared scalar/template bundle, materializes refcounted side objects, and then finalizes the assembled record.
fresh_helper_structure.evidence_source: Fresh headless no-analysis dumps in this pass on rs2quick26.

evidence.observation: stores the caller-provided dword into [RCX] and preserves RDX in RBX, making RBX the incoming string-like label argument for the rest of the helper.
initializes an inline short-string object at [RCX +0x8], then copies the incoming caller string into it via 0x14002F7C0.
seeds a fixed scalar/template bundle on RSI: +0x74 = 0x40000000, +0x70 = 0x3f800000, +0x60 = 1, +0x68 = 0, +0x78 = 0, +0x80 = 0, +0xC0 = 0.
writes fixed pointer 0x140C38628 into [RSI +0x58], establishing a stable shared template/default pointer inside every record this helper builds.
selects between two fixed pointer roots, 0x140BA0248 and 0x140B74258, based on whether [RSI] == 1, but keeps 0x140C38628 constant across both branches.
conditionally replaces the temporary string object with literal 0x140B5E03C = '-' when the stack byte at [RSP +0x1B0] is non-zero.
passes the temporary label string, a mode bit derived from [RSI] == 1, and caller-provided RDI into helper 0x14067C1F0.
stores the returned pair from 0x14067C1F0 into [RSI +0x40] and [RSI +0x48].
releases the prior [RSI +0x40] refcounted object and a second temporary object rooted at [RSP +0x30], showing the helper owns refcounted side objects rather than only raw strings.
calls 0x140679A30 as the final record-finishing step before returning RSI.

shared_pointer_classification.statement: 0x140C38628 is a shared fixed template/default pointer seeded by 0x140679500 and reused by multiple unrelated constructors, not a 0x19E90-specific payload string.

supporting_hits.observation: 0x140679500 writes 0x140C38628 into [RSI +0x58] while assembling the same label/helper record family used by the 0x19E90 'totp'/'login' path.
a second nearby record builder seeds the same pointer into [RSI +0x58] while assembling the same scalar block shape.
another constructor path loads 0x140C38628 while building a separate RBX-rooted object with matching scalar defaults.
yet another builder stores 0x140C38628 into [RBX +0x70], proving the pointer survives outside the narrow 0x19E90/login-preparation family.
the newer 0x19E90 template/config consumer writes the same pointer into [RAX +0x18], tying that local record to the shared helper/default-pointer family.

one_off_pointer_status.statement: 0x140B67918 remains a one-off local fixed pointer within the 0x1400CCC87 record builder. Fresh operand scans still only surface the single LEA at 0x1400CCC91, so its semantics remain unresolved.

relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-client-19e90-totp-login-label-cons: Strengthens the earlier artifact by proving that the called helper 0x140679500 is a concrete record builder with a shared fixed pointer at +0x58 and a conditional '-' fallback, rather than an opaque formatting shim.
relationship_to_previous_milestones.login-client-19e90-lifetime-reset-and-te: Explains why 0x1400CCC87 belongs in the same broader object family as the earlier label-driven helper path: both reuse 0x140C38628 as a shared template/default pointer inside structurally similar seeded records.

bounded_inference.statement: The 0x19E90-driven 'totp'/'login' path is building richer labeled entry records than previously stated. Those records include a copied label string, a shared default/template pointer (0x140C38628), scalar defaults, and refcounted side objects finalized through 0x140679A30; the later 0x1400CCC87 consumer appears to seed a related but shorter local record using the same fixed pointer family.

next_targets: Name helpers 0x14067C1F0 and 0x140679A30 so the exact meaning of the refcounted side objects at +0x40/+0x48 can be classified.
Determine what the two alternate fixed roots 0x140BA0248 and 0x140B74258 represent and how the [RSI] == 1 selector affects the built record.
Keep the reset-side 0x1400255B6 recovery on the backlog, but prioritize the shared record family while the helper windows are dumping cleanly.
